data.title: Citi Reiterates Hold on AMD, Keeps Neutral Stance with Unchanged $248 Price Target After Sharp Rally
data.description:
Atif Malik, an analyst from Citi, maintained the Hold rating on Advanced Micro Devices. The associated price target remains the same with $248.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Atif Malik has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors around AMD’s recent share performance and shifting risk‑reward. He notes that the stock has already rallied sharply, rising more than 58% since early April, largely in anticipation of stronger demand and better pricing for the company’s CPU products, which limits additional near‑term upside.

Given that much of the optimism on improved fundamentals now appears embedded in the current valuation, Malik no longer sees a distinct short‑term catalyst that would justify a more bullish stance. As a result, he removes AMD from Citi’s positive 30‑day catalyst watch and reiterates a neutral positioning, keeping his price target unchanged at $248, which aligns with this more balanced outlook.

In another report released today, Northland Securities also downgraded the stock to a Hold with a $260.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Fiera Capital Kept at Hold as Analyst Maintains $7.50 Price Target Amid Persistent Net Outflows
data.description:
Etienne Ricard, an analyst from BMO Capital, maintained the Hold rating on Fiera Capital A. The associated price target remains the same with C$7.50.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Etienne Ricard has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Fiera Capital’s fundamentals and current market positioning. He notes that, despite the shares looking inexpensive on a free cash flow basis, persistent net outflows in core public market strategies and only gradually improving trends in PineStone sub-advised assets limit the likelihood of a swift valuation re-rating.

He also emphasizes that his longer-term earnings forecasts have edged lower following recent AUM developments, reinforcing a cautious stance. While the downside appears partly cushioned by an attractive yield, buybacks, and a diversified mix across public and private markets, the absence of clear, near-term catalysts and the ongoing need for sustainably positive net flows support maintaining a Hold, with the price target kept unchanged at $7.50.

According to TipRanks, Ricard is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 24.1% and a 65.96% success rate. Ricard covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as Fiera Capital A, goeasy, and Sprott.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
